papers.title: Dense Retrieval for Scientific Question Answering

chunks.text: Dense retrieval represents queries and documents in an embedding space, then retrieves nearest neighbors by vector similarity.
Negative sampling teaches a model to distinguish relevant examples from non-relevant alternatives during contrastive training.
Contrastive learning improves representations by pulling positive pairs together and pushing negative pairs apart.
A RAG pipeline retrieves external evidence and conditions generation on that evidence to reduce unsupported answers.
Evaluation of RAG checks whether generated answers are grounded in retrieved evidence and flags unsupported (hallucinated) claims.

quiz_items.question: Dense retrieval mainly compares documents using what representation?
What role do negatives play in contrastive training?
What happens to positive pairs in contrastive learning?
Why does RAG retrieve evidence before generation?
quiz_items.answer_key: to condition generation on external evidence

quiz_items.question: What does RAG evaluation primarily check for?
